title: Race-Lap Query Ranking Expert
summary: The real decision is often relative: among the 20 cars on the current lap, which ones are most likely to pit next.
rationale: The real decision is often relative: among the 20 cars on the current lap, which ones are most likely to pit next. CatBoost and LightGBM both expose query/ranking objectives that fit this framing better than pure independent binary classification.
implementation_hint: Create a specialist where each query is Race_Year x LapNumber and positives are drivers with PitNextLap=1 on that lap. Train CatBoost QueryCrossEntropy/YetiRank or LightGBM LambdaRank on field-relative features only: lap-wise percentiles, distance from race-lap medians, stint age rank, compound rarity on that lap, and legality/finish-window state. Blend OOF scores with the existing classifier.
expected_effect: Can sharpen within-lap ordering and improve AUC when multiple cars share similar absolute tyre-life features but only a few are truly in the pit window.
risk: Queries with zero or many positives can be noisy; performance depends on careful grouping and excluding absolute leak-prone race identifiers from the ranker.
